title: specgen,wasm: switch to crun-wasm wherever applicable
body:
Whenever image has `arch` and `os` configured for `wasm/wasi` switch to `crun-wasm` as extension if applicable, following will only work if `podman` is using `crun` as default runtime.

[NO NEW TEST NEEDED]
[NO TESTS NEEDED]
A test for this can we added when `crun-wasm` package is part of the CI.

```release-note
specgen,wasm: switch to crun-wasm if image is a webassembly image
```

comments.body:
the first thing to sort out is how the `containers.conf` file will look like.

Now we have:

```toml
[engine.runtimes]
#crun = [
#  "/usr/bin/crun",
#  "/usr/sbin/crun",
#  "/usr/local/bin/crun",
#  "/usr/local/sbin/crun",
#  "/sbin/crun",
#  "/bin/crun",
#  "/run/current-system/sw/bin/crun",
#]

#kata = [
#  "/usr/bin/kata-runtime",
#  "/usr/sbin/kata-runtime",
#  "/usr/local/bin/kata-runtime",
#  "/usr/local/sbin/kata-runtime",
#  "/sbin/kata-runtime",
#  "/bin/kata-runtime",
#  "/usr/bin/kata-qemu",
#  "/usr/bin/kata-fc",
#]

#runc = [
#  "/usr/bin/runc",
#  "/usr/sbin/runc",
#  "/usr/local/bin/runc",
#  "/usr/local/sbin/runc",
#  "/sbin/runc",
#  "/bin/runc",
#  "/usr/lib/cri-o-runc/sbin/runc",
#]

#runsc = [
#  "/usr/bin/runsc",
#  "/usr/sbin/runsc",
#  "/usr/local/bin/runsc",
#  "/usr/local/sbin/runsc",
#  "/bin/runsc",
#  "/sbin/runsc",
#  "/run/current-system/sw/bin/runsc",
#]

#krun = [
#  "/usr/bin/krun",
#  "/usr/local/bin/krun",
#]
....
```

How can we extend it to specify also an arch for an OCI runtime?

@giuseppe Could we add a new map maybe something like ?

```toml
platformToOCI = [{ platform = "wasm/wasi", runtime_name = "crun-wasm" },
              { platform = "windows/amd64", runtime_name = "crun-windows" }]
 ```
@mheon Could you check the new push if its any better than the last one ?
Added settings to `containers.conf` this needs following PR https://github.com/containers/common/pull/1225
Also: What's the difference between crun and crun-wasm? I thought crun had WASM capability built in?

> Also: What's the difference between crun and crun-wasm? I thought crun had WASM capability built in?

`crun-wasm` delegates all the workload directly to crun's wasm handler and not treats it as native linux container.
Will rebase once upstream https://github.com/containers/common/pull/1225 is merged.
@flouthoc the common got merged, please rebase.
@vrothberg Created an Issue and assigned to myself, will fix it very soon https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/16501
